libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the default in-memory Quartz JobStore; no Docker service is needed. Prefer a small ApplicationContextRunner test that registers a simple JobDetail/Trigger or verifies SchedulerFactoryBean/Scheduler creation, and ensure the Spring context or scheduler is closed after each run. Avoid switching to spring.quartz.job-store-type=jdbc unless the test also supplies an embedded DataSource and schema initialization.

deterministic_setup.reason: The artifact contains QuartzAutoConfiguration and related auto-configuration classes, but its resolved dependencies do not include the core Boot auto-configuration APIs needed to exercise them in the normal Spring Boot style.

Spring Boot auto-configuration modules are typically tested with ApplicationContextRunner, which is provided by spring-boot-test and gives meaningful lightweight coverage without launching a full application.

risks: The artifact also contains optional actuator endpoint classes; endpoint-specific coverage may require spring-boot-actuator, but core Quartz scheduler auto-configuration coverage does not.
Quartz starts scheduler threads when auto-startup is enabled, so tests must close the context or scheduler to avoid hangs.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Spring Boot's Quartz support is normally exercised as Boot auto-configuration; the resolved artifact brings Quartz and Spring context support but not Boot auto-configuration/test support used for meaningful context-based coverage.
